name: Titan's Rest â€” Ralven's Gate
description: The fortified main entrance to Titan's Rest is flanked by two stone pillars carved with the Gilded Standard Company's founding motto: 'Your life is worth more than your master.' Three centuries of Farborn hands have worn the words smooth at touching-height, a habit begun by children learning to read and never stopped. The gate's iron portcullis is raised in peacetime, and the guards who flank it wear the Gilded's sand-gold livery with the easy confidence of soldiers who have never lost a gate.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Gate Road
description: A broad paved road leads from Ralven's Gate up the Lone Titan's western slope toward the city's commercial heart, its surface of fitted dark stone worn to a high polish by three centuries of boots and hooves. Terraced farmland rises on both sides behind low stone walls, and the smell of Titanbreed horse-sweat and mountain herbs is the constant background of Titan's Rest. Farborn civilians move with the brisk purposefulness of a culture that was built by soldiers.

name: Titan's Rest â€” The Bazaar (South Hall)
description: The southern hall of the Bazaar is dominated by the horse-trading operations that make Titan's Rest the most consequential single market in the Deepwater Marches. Titanbreed hybrids are exhibited in rope enclosures along the southern wall â€” animals of extraordinary size, calm, and apparent intelligence whose bloodlines descend from three centuries of careful breeding on the Lone Titan's slopes. Buyers from as far as the Twin Rivers arrive with letters of credit and leave with horses that will define their cavalry for a generation.

name: Titan's Rest â€” The Bazaar (North Hall)
description: The northern hall of the Bazaar handles the goods that flow in and out of Titan's Rest in exchange for its horses â€” steppe-wool, mountain herbs, Titansteel horseshoes, and the intelligence that the Farborn's far-ranging trade caravans collect as a byproduct of their commercial operations. Merchants from every settled culture in Oreka maintain buying offices here; the Bazaar's multilingual commerce has produced a trade-pidgin that experienced travelers recognize as a reliable indicator of how far south they have come.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Trade Quarter (East)
description: The trade quarter surrounding the Bazaar is a dense neighborhood of counting-houses, warehouses, and factor's offices where the commercial machinery that supports Titan's Rest's horse economy operates continuously. Farborn accountants and Halfling brokers work alongside each other in a professional partnership of long standing â€” the Halfling traders who route goods through Titan's Rest provide the caravan infrastructure that the Farborn heavy cavalry could not efficiently maintain themselves.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Trade Quarter (West)
description: The western trade quarter provides support services for the Bazaar's horse operations â€” farriers, tack-makers, veterinary practitioners, and the specialized Titansteel smiths whose horseshoes are exported as a product in their own right. Titansteel is not a true separate metal â€” it is standard iron worked to a proprietary Farborn process that increases its durability and reduces its weight â€” but the Farborn guard the process closely enough that 'Titansteel' has become a recognized premium designation in markets they have never directly entered.

name: Titan's Rest â€” The Gilded Yards (Entrance)
description: The entrance to the Gilded Yards training ground is marked by a gate of ironwood hung with the company's original standard â€” gilded thread on sand-colored cloth, depicting a horse in full gallop. The standard is not the original (that is preserved in the Captain-General's hall) but a faithful copy renewed every generation. Visitors may observe training from a viewing platform just inside the gate but may not enter the training ground itself without escort.

name: Titan's Rest â€” The Gilded Yards (Training Ground)
description: The training ground is an expanse of packed earth on a natural terrace of the Lone Titan's slope, large enough for full cavalry formations to maneuver. Light cavalry practice mounted archery at targets along the eastern edge while Dusk Guard heavy cavalry drill formation charges across the central ground, their Titanbreed mounts responding to leg and weight cues with a sensitivity that makes the riders appear almost motionless during complex maneuvers. The quality of horsemanship here is the finest observable anywhere in Oreka.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Cavalry Fields (North)
description: The northern cavalry fields extend up the Lone Titan's slope where the terrain's natural variation provides training challenges impossible to replicate on flat ground. Farborn riders work young Titanbreeds through rougher terrain here â€” rocky sections, stream crossings, and slope transitions that test both horse and rider's confidence in uncertain footing. The relationship between a Farborn cavalry officer and their Titanbreed mount develops over years of shared work, and this is where that relationship is built.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Cavalry Fields (West Pastures)
description: The western pastures are where Titanbreed horses are kept when not in training â€” broad terraced grassland where the mountain herbs that give Titanbreed animals their distinctive health and temperament grow naturally. The herd here at any given time numbers two to three hundred horses in various stages of training, from foals to fully certified cavalry mounts awaiting sale. Two Titanborne war-steeds â€” the sacred purebloods never for sale â€” are kept in a separate enclosure and are visible from the observation fence.

name: Titan's Rest â€” East Residential (Lower)
description: The lower eastern residential terraces house Titan's Rest's working population â€” the grooms, smiths, provisioners, and support tradespeople whose labor maintains the city's horse economy. The buildings are stone-block construction of Farborn design: practical, thick-walled, and built with the assumption of permanence that distinguishes settler architecture from garrison architecture. Children play in the stepped lanes between terrace levels, and the sound of domestic activity is comfortable and continuous.

name: Titan's Rest â€” East Residential (Upper)
description: The upper eastern residences are home to Titan's Rest's professional class â€” cavalry officers, senior traders, and the administrative staff of the Captain-General's government. The buildings are somewhat larger and the lanes between them wider, with small gardens cut into the terrace walls that grow herbs and a few fruit trees adapted to mountain altitude. The view east from this level looks across the jungle canopy to the horizon of the Eternal Steppe, a panorama of the two worlds the Farborn straddle.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Summit Road
description: A stone-paved road ascends the Lone Titan's upper slope from the residential quarter toward the Summit Shrine. The road is steeper than the lower city's terraced streets, and its surface is fitted with drainage channels and anti-slip scoring. Farborn pilgrims â€” the word is approximate; the Farborn do not worship Oreka's Elemental Lords but maintain a reverence for the mountain that functions similarly â€” make this ascent regularly and in silence.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Summit Shrine Approach
description: The final approach to the Summit Shrine crosses a section of bare mountain rock where the Lone Titan's deep stone is exposed and the Giant rune-circle's influence is most palpable. Even Farborn visitors who have no Kin-sense and no elemental attunement report a physical sensation here â€” a warmth in the stone that is not solar heat, a steadiness underfoot that feels guided rather than geological. Three centuries of Farborn habitation have not produced an explanation, but they have produced a very deep comfort with the mystery.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Summit Shrine
description: The Giant-era rune-circle at the Lone Titan's peak pulses with faint amber light at all hours, its carved channels deep enough that centuries of mountain weather have not blurred them. The Farborn have added no structures here â€” a deliberate choice, honoring what the mountain gave them by leaving it unchanged. The Founder's Ride festival ends here each year, with the surviving riders of the day's cavalry display ascending on foot to stand in the circle's glow in silence. The metaphysical protection this site provides against Domnathar scrying is not something the Farborn know in explicit terms; they know only that the mountain hides them.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Dusk Guard Barracks
description: The Farborn heavy cavalry â€” the Dusk Guards â€” are housed in a barracks complex adjacent to the Gilded Yards where their Titanbreed warhorses can be reached at a run. The barracks itself is built to the heavy cavalry's physical requirements: wide corridors for armored movement, weapon-racks at appropriate heights, and a central hall large enough to brief a full company standing with horses. The Dusk Guards are the Captain-General's primary force-projection tool and they are aware of it.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Gilded Company Barracks
description: The light cavalry barracks houses the Gilded proper â€” the descendants of the original Gilded Standard Company who serve as Titan's Rest's mobile defense and long-range patrol force. The atmosphere here is lighter than the Dusk Guard barracks, with the informal ease of soldiers who spend more time in the field than at garrison. Trophies from three centuries of Marches patrols cover the mess-hall walls, and the duty roster shows rotation schedules that extend weeks into the Deepwood and steppe margins.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Captain-General's Hall
description: Captain-General Maren Duskharrow governs Titan's Rest from this stone hall built on the Lone Titan's upper terrace, its walls hung with the original Gilded Standard and the personal standards of every Captain-General since Ralven. The governance here is by merit and acclamation â€” the Farborn have no hereditary aristocracy, a fact that Ralven's motto makes explicit. Maren's working style is military in its directness: briefings are factual, decisions are quick, and the door is open to anyone with information the Captain-General needs.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Halfling Quarter
description: Titan's Rest's Halfling residents â€” five percent of the population, concentrated in the lower eastern quarter â€” maintain a neighborhood that serves simultaneously as a cultural enclave and a commercial hub for the small-goods trade that complements the city's horse economy. Halfling cooking smells better than the Farborn grain-and-meat standard, a fact that has resulted in a ring of Halfling food stalls around the quarter's edge that serves the entire city's appetite for variety.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Mixed Traders' Row
description: The two percent of Titan's Rest's population that is neither Farborn nor Halfling concentrates here in a traders' row where all Kin cultures are represented in small numbers. Kovaka stone-workers, Eruskan brokers, and an occasional Pekakarlik hydraulics engineer maintain offices and workshops in this row, providing skills and connections that the Farborn community cannot fully supply internally. The row has a diplomatic function as well â€” it is the space where non-Farborn visitors are initially received.

name: Titan's Rest â€” West Gate
description: The western gate of Titan's Rest faces the Ash Trails and the deep Deepwood beyond, and its security posture reflects the fact that nothing organized and friendly comes from that direction. The gate is manned at full strength regardless of peacetime conditions, and a pair of Gilded scouts maintains a standing patrol circuit on the jungle margin half a mile out. Caravans returning from Ash Trail routes enter here, and their intelligence about trail conditions is debriefed at the gate before they proceed to the Bazaar.

name: Titan's Rest â€” West Residential
description: The western residential quarter is less fashionable than the eastern terraces â€” closer to the West Gate and therefore to whatever arrives from the jungle. The buildings are well-maintained but the residents are those who chose or needed cheaper accommodation: junior cavalry officers, newly arrived traders, and a population of mixed-Kin workers whose presence the city tolerates with the pragmatism that distinguishes Farborn social attitudes from those of the northern Kin cultures.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Provisioner's District
description: The provisioner's district supplies Titan's Rest's cavalry operations with everything a horse-based military civilization requires: grain, fodder, tack, leather, iron fittings, and the specialized Titanbreed supplement feeds that the horse breeders have developed to optimize herd health at mountain altitude. The district's warehouses are large enough to support a siege of several months â€” a calculation the Farborn founders made explicitly in the city's original layout.

name: Titan's Rest â€” South Gate & Steppe Road
description: The southern gate opens toward the Eternal Steppe and the Great Pass beyond, the primary connection between Titan's Rest and the wider world east of the Marches. Farborn trade caravans depart through this gate for every region of Oreka, and the Captain-General's intelligence network formally begins its information gathering here â€” every returning caravan is expected to report what it observed. The road south is well-maintained for the first five miles, then becomes steppe.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Healer's House
description: The Farborn maintain a healer's house staffed by a combination of Farborn military surgeons and whatever Kin healers can be retained through competitive wages. The surgical practice here is notably advanced for a frontier city â€” three centuries of cavalry warfare have produced considerable expertise in trauma medicine. The healer's house also serves as Titan's Rest's veterinary hospital, since the Titanbreed horses represent enough economic value to justify the same standard of medical care as the cavalry riders.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Founders' Monument
description: A carved stone monument at the city's geographic center depicts Captain Ralven Duskharrow in the moment of the breakaway at Dark Dawn â€” not the heroic pose of official commemoration but a figure in motion, one hand raised in the signal to withdraw, looking back over his shoulder at the chaos behind. The sculptor's choice to show Ralven's expression as uncertain rather than triumphant has been controversial across every generation of Farborn since. Most have decided it is more honest than the alternative.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Archive & Records Hall
description: The Farborn maintain meticulous records â€” a military culture's habit extended to civil administration â€” in this stone archive adjacent to the Captain-General's hall. Three centuries of census data, trade records, patrol reports, and horse-breeding genealogies are stored here in a system organized by a Halfling archivist whose family has held the position for six generations. The archive's horse-genealogy records alone are worth a substantial ransom to competing horse-breeders.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Forge Quarter
description: The forge quarter produces the Titansteel horseshoes, iron fittings, and weapons that Titan's Rest's cavalry requires. The smiths here are Farborn by culture and training, their techniques a three-century evolution of whatever forge knowledge the original Company brought from their home world, modified by contact with Rarozhki methods through trade. The results are distinctive â€” not Embersteel, not standard iron, but something that experienced metallurgists find genuinely interesting.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Lower Terrace Market
description: A second market below the Bazaar handles everyday goods â€” food, clothing, tools, and the small commerce of daily life. Less impressive than the Bazaar's horse-trade, but more representative of how most of Titan's Rest's twenty-eight thousand inhabitants actually spend their days. Halfling food vendors are reliably the busiest stalls, and the fresh-pressed mountain herb oils they sell are carried back north by every departing caravan.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Gilded Company Mess Hall
description: The original Gilded Standard Company's mess tradition â€” officers and soldiers eat the same food at the same tables â€” has been maintained for three centuries in this long hall adjacent to the company barracks. The egalitarianism is not merely symbolic; Ralven's founding principle that no life has more intrinsic value than another extends to the table. Civilian visitors who are invited to eat here find the informality between ranks startling until they realize it is entirely genuine.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Observation Platform
description: A platform on the Lone Titan's exposed western face gives unobstructed views across the Deepwood canopy and, on clear days, the distant glint of the Apelian Sea. Gilded scouts use this as a long-range observation post when reports of activity in the jungle margin require assessment. The platform is built of the same dark stone as the mountain itself and is effectively invisible from below â€” a design choice that was deliberate.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Stablemaster's Complex
description: The largest single structure in Titan's Rest is not the Captain-General's hall but the stablemaster's complex â€” a network of covered stalls, training corrals, and breeding facilities that houses the horses closest to certification for sale. The stablemaster, a heavyset Farborn woman named Essa Graytread, manages a staff of forty and maintains records on every animal in her care with a thoroughness that the archive considers her professional peer.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Titanborne Enclosure
description: The two Titanborne war-steeds â€” the sacred purebloods, never for sale, displayed at every major fair as living proof of the bloodline's authenticity â€” are kept in a separate enclosure of ironwood and Titansteel fittings. The animals are magnificent: larger than standard Titanbreeds, with the faint elemental resonance that the Farborn call 'mountain-touched' and scholars of Kin-sense call something considerably more interesting. No unauthorized person enters the enclosure; the penalty is the Captain-General's personal attention.

name: Titan's Rest â€” Duskharrow Family Quarters
description: The Duskharrow family â€” descendants of Captain Ralven, the only family in Titan's Rest with a hereditary distinction â€” occupies quarters adjacent to the Captain-General's hall. The distinction is acknowledged but not privileged: Duskharrows serve in the Gilded or the Dusk Guards as their merit warrants, and the current Captain-General Maren Duskharrow holds her position by election, not lineage. The quarters themselves are no larger or better appointed than those of any senior officer.

name: Tidewell â€” Stiltmarket (Upper Deck)
description: The upper deck of the Stiltmarket rises above the Apelian Sea's high-water line on ironwood stilts sunk into the lakebed, its planking sound enough that the creak underfoot is structural confidence rather than warning. Lake pearl dealers, dried-fish merchants, and medicinal herb traders occupy permanent stalls whose awnings are the first thing approaching visitors see above the mangrove line. Harbormaster Enna Farshore's office occupies the market's northeast corner with deliberate visibility.

name: Tidewell â€” Stiltmarket (Lower Deck)
description: The lower deck of the Stiltmarket sits at the water line and submerges to the knee at high water, when trade shifts to canoe-accessible upper levels and the lower stalls are cleared of goods as a routine precaution. At normal water levels the lower deck handles bulk goods â€” crates of tidal crops, barrels of eel-oil, and the large medicinal reed bundles that require wide clearance to carry. The sound of the Apelian lapping against the pilings is constant and hypnotic.

name: Tidewell â€” Portage Yard
description: The Portage Yard is the southern terminus of the Sea Falls portage route â€” the infrastructure link that makes Tidewell the most strategically important settlement in the Deepwater Marches. Pekakarlik-maintained docks line the yard's waterfront, and overland wagons queue at the transfer point where lake cargo is reloaded for the climb to the portage bypass. Harbormaster Enna Farshore's control of the portage fees is the source of her political power and the reason every faction in the Marches maintains a civil relationship with Tidewell.

name: Tidewell â€” Eelglow Wharf
description: The Eelglow Wharf processes and bottles the luminescent eel-oil that is the Marches' most valuable export by volume. The wharf glows faintly at all hours â€” visible from several miles across the Apelian on moonless nights, a landmark used by lake traffic the way a lighthouse functions elsewhere. The rendering process is straightforward but requires skilled operators to produce oil of consistent luminescence quality, and the Halfling families who run the wharf guard their process carefully.

name: Tidewell â€” Tideshrine
description: The Tideshrine to the Lady of the Sea is built on a floating platform moored to the Stiltmarket's western pilings, rising and falling with the Apelian's seasonal water level. The shrine's deck is carved with wave-reliefs and set with pearl offerings that replace the old as new pilgrims arrive. Water magic is amplified here, and the shrine's priests maintain a tradition of offerings to calm the water serpents that hunt the shallows â€” the tradition is old enough that it is impossible to say whether it works because of magical effect or accumulated serpent habit.

name: Tidewell â€” Lower Dock District
description: The working docks east of the Stiltmarket handle the fishing fleet that provides Tidewell with its primary protein supply. Halfling and Pasua fishing craft in a dozen configurations tie up here in the predawn dark and return loaded by mid-morning. The catch is processed immediately at the dock-side cleaning stations and the waste returned to the lake â€” a Tidewell custom that the pearl-divers downstream have periodically objected to without success.

name: Tidewell â€” Reed Market
description: The medicinal reeds harvested from the Verdant Shoals are dried, graded, and traded in this specialized market west of the Stiltmarket's main deck. Reed quality varies enormously by harvest location and season, and the graders who work here are among the most specialized professionals in the Marches. The dried reed bundles are exported primarily north through the portage route, where they command strong prices from the apothecaries of the Kinsweave and Twin Rivers regions.

name: Tidewell â€” Portage Road Junction
description: The road from the Portage Yard joins the main track toward the Sea Falls here, and the traffic at this junction is the most reliable indicator of trade conditions across the entire Marches. Heavy portage traffic means goods flowing north; light traffic means disruption somewhere on the route. The junction has a waystation where portage workers rest between shifts and caravan masters compare notes on trail conditions north and south.

name: Tidewell â€” Harbormaster's Hall
description: Harbormaster Enna Farshore runs Tidewell's trade council from this hall, its walls covered with the same kind of meticulous route and cargo records that the Pekakarlik Flowkeepers maintain for river systems. Enna's political style is the pragmatism of someone who knows that her authority rests entirely on the portage fees and the goodwill of the traders who pay them â€” she is fair, she is consistent, and she is not naive about the connection between those qualities and her continued relevance.

name: Tidewell â€” North Shore Residences
description: The permanent residential population of Tidewell lives primarily on the north shore above the flood line, in a neighborhood of Halfling-built stilt-houses connected by plank walkways. The architecture is the Halfling maritime style â€” lightweight, adaptable, designed to be rebuilt after flood damage without complete loss â€” and the neighborhood has in fact been rebuilt in sections several times across Tidewell's history without ever ceasing to function as a living community.

name: Tidewell â€” South Shore & Fisher Camps
description: The south shore is less developed than the north â€” a mix of permanent fisher camps and seasonal camps for pearl-divers who operate the Verdant Shoals during the diving season. The camps have a temporary feel that belies their age: some of these family campsites have been in continuous use for a hundred years by the same Halfling lineages, but Halfling maritime culture does not build for permanence when it can build for adaptability.

name: Tidewell â€” Luminous Night Festival Grounds
description: The Luminous Night festival â€” floating lanterns released on the Apelian at the wet season's end â€” is organized and launched from this prepared ground at the lake's edge. Outside festival season the ground serves as a community gathering space and informal market. The posts from which the lantern-stringing is hung stand year-round, and a permanent display of previous years' lantern-making materials lines one wall â€” an informal archive of the festival's artistic evolution.

name: Tidewell â€” Pasua Quarter
description: Tidewell's thirty percent Pasua Elf population maintains a quarter on the settlement's eastern edge where the jungle margin comes closest to the lake. Pasua culture in the Marches is maritime rather than forest-focused, a regional adaptation of the Wind Lord's elves who took to the Apelian's warm waters generations ago. Their long canoes are distinctive â€” narrow, fast, and remarkably stable in the lake chop â€” and their water-knowledge supplements Tidewell's Halfling boatwright tradition in ways both communities value.

name: Tidewell â€” Portage Climb (Lower Stage)
description: The first stage of the portage climb from Tidewell toward the Sea Falls is the steepest section, requiring teams of draft animals working in relay to move heavy cargo up the initial grade. The road surface has been improved repeatedly by Pekakarlik engineers hired by Tidewell's trade council â€” the current surface of dressed stone with drainage channels and passing-places represents several generations of incremental improvement over what was originally a jungle trail.

name: Tidewell â€” Healer's Cottage
description: A Halfling healer named Fern Shallowbrook maintains this cottage at the north shore residential area's edge, providing medical care to Tidewell's population with a combination of traditional Halfling herb-lore and practical surgery developed for a maritime community's typical injuries. The cottage keeps a stock of Marches medicinal herbs that would be the envy of a northern apothecary, sourced directly from the Verdant Shoals and the Canopy Hold trading connection.

name: Tidewell â€” Lake Shore Path (East)
description: A path follows the Apelian's north shore eastward from Tidewell, skirting the mangrove margins where the lake meets the jungle. Pearl-divers use the path to reach their working grounds in the Verdant Shoals, and Pasua canoe-stations are located along it at intervals. The path is navigable but requires local knowledge of which sections flood reliably during wet season and which are year-round passage.

name: Canopy Hold â€” Rootwall Exterior
description: The living fortification of Canopy Hold's Rootwall is visible from two hundred yards out â€” a dense weave of ironwood trunk and trained vine that presents no gap wider than a handspan to the outside world. The wall is alive: new growth fills any damage within weeks, and the Kovaka earth-druids who maintain it have shaped it over decades into something between architecture and organism. From the jungle side it looks like a natural thicket. The distinction is real only if you try to enter.

name: Canopy Hold â€” Rootwall Gate
description: The only gate through the Rootwall is a tunnel of trained vine whose opening is controlled by a Kovaka gate-warden stationed in a platform above. The tunnel is wide enough for two people abreast, and the vines overhead are thick enough that no light penetrates â€” visitors enter in complete darkness for about ten paces before the inner side opens onto the settlement's base level. The gate-warden's challenge is spoken in Kovaka Elvish first; Common second.

name: Canopy Hold â€” Base Level
description: The base level of Canopy Hold, inside the Rootwall, is a dim space of massive tree-trunks whose bases are wider than houses and whose bark has been carved with the settlement's founding history in the angular Kovaka script-style. Platform access ladders rise at each major trunk, and rope-and-plank walkways stretch overhead at multiple heights. The ambient sound is the perpetual drip of filtered rain from the canopy far above and the creak of platforms swaying in the upper wind.

name: Canopy Hold â€” First Platform Level
description: The first platform level, forty feet above the forest floor, is where most of Canopy Hold's daily commerce and community life occurs. Platforms of ironwood plank connected by vine-bridges span the gaps between the settlement's major trees, and the entire level moves gently in the wind in a way that residents long since stopped noticing. Kovaka and Pasua elves move between platforms with the casualness of people for whom forty-foot drops are simply geography.

name: Canopy Hold â€” Windweave Platform
description: The Windweave Platform, at eighty feet elevation, is where Pasua elves weave the tropical wind-silk from moth cocoons harvested in the upper canopy. The platform is open to the sky at this level â€” below the main canopy but above most of the settlement's other platforms â€” and catches the warm updrafts that characterize the Marches' humid climate. The wind-silk produced here is lighter than northern varieties and commands premium prices; bolts in production are visible on dozens of small looms worked by Pasua weavers who do not pause for visitors.

name: Canopy Hold â€” Shieldfather's Hall
description: Shieldfather Theren Deeproot's command hall occupies a platform at the settlement's military center, reinforced with ironwood planking three layers deep and surrounded by an unobstructed sightline that prevents approach without detection. Theren's tactical maps of the Deepwood cover the hall's interior surfaces â€” hand-drawn by rangers over decades, updated whenever new information arrives. The Shieldfather is usually here when not on patrol, and his door is open to anyone with information about Deceiver activity.

name: Canopy Hold â€” Kovaka Warriors' Platform
description: The Kovaka warriors who form Canopy Hold's primary defensive force are housed on this reinforced platform where weapons and armor can be stored without the weight concerns that limit more lightly-built Pasua platforms. Kovaka rangers clean weapons and drill hand-to-hand combat techniques on the platform's central training space in the deliberate, unhurried manner of warriors who know that technique preserved through patience is more reliable than technique drilled under pressure.

name: Canopy Hold â€” Pasua Residential Platform
description: The Pasua Elf majority of Canopy Hold lives on a cluster of lighter platforms in the settlement's western section, their homes the woven-vine and leaf-fabric structures that Pasua culture has refined for forest living over millennia. Wind-silk partitions provide privacy in the open-air platform spaces, and the sounds of Pasua instruments â€” small string instruments and hollow-wood percussion â€” drift through the settlement from this platform at most hours.

name: Canopy Hold â€” East Gate & Ladder
description: The eastern access of Canopy Hold is a rope-and-wood ladder descending the Rootwall's interior face to a concealed exit in the wall's base â€” used primarily by ranger patrols rather than general traffic. The exit point on the exterior is disguised as natural growth and known only to Canopy Hold's warriors and the Shieldfather's staff. It is the settlement's emergency exit and its primary intelligence-gathering egress.

name: Canopy Hold â€” Deepwatch Spire
description: The Deepwatch Spire is built into the crown of the settlement's largest sky-oak, its observation platform rising above the canopy at one hundred and eighty feet â€” high enough to see movement across the Deepwood canopy for five miles in clear conditions. Kovaka rangers staff it in continuous shifts, their trained hawk companions circling the observation zone in patterns that extend effective surveillance to seven miles. The view from the spire at dawn, when the canopy mist is burning off and the Apelian's distant surface catches the first light, is spoken of by those who have seen it as one of the most beautiful things in Oreka.

name: Canopy Hold â€” Na'wasua Scholars' Platform
description: Canopy Hold's ten percent Na'wasua Elf population maintains a small scholars' platform where the study of the Deepwood's ancient rune-circles and Giant ruins is conducted with characteristic Na'wasua methodological care. The platform is hung with observational instruments and star-charts â€” the Na'wasua astronomical tradition continues even in the dense jungle where star observation requires ascending above the canopy â€” and the shelves of stone-tablet records are organized in the Na'wasua archival system.

name: Canopy Hold â€” Rootsong Hollow
description: The natural amphitheater formed by exposed root systems at the forest floor serves as Canopy Hold's sacred and social gathering space. The root walls of ancient sky-oaks form curved tiers that seat two hundred comfortably, and the hollow amplifies sound in the same way it amplifies air magic â€” a Pasua druid working gust-of-wind here affects an area twice the normal spell radius. The annual Rootsong Festival fills this space with the drumming ceremony that honors the deep jungle's spirits, but the hollow is used for councils and significant community decisions year-round.

name: Canopy Hold â€” Herb Garden Platform
description: An unusual feature in an arboreal settlement, the herb garden platform is a series of soil-filled ironwood boxes suspended between major trunks at mid-height, where sufficient light reaches through the canopy gaps for cultivation. Halfling herbalists who live in Canopy Hold maintain the garden, growing both medicinal herbs for export and the cooking ingredients that vary the settlement's otherwise monotonous forest-food diet. The herbs grown here are different species from the desert varieties â€” the Marches pharmacopoeia is its own discipline.

name: Thornwall â€” The Palisade Gate
description: The outer ring of Thornwall's defenses is a double row of thorn-crusted logs and sharpened stakes that constitute one of the most effective low-technology fortifications in the Marches. The gate is a goblin-engineered lift-door of exceptional cunning â€” a wooden portcullis whose counterweight system allows a single goblin to raise it against a force that would require ten humans. The gate-guard is always two goblins and two wargs, and neither species operates the gate without the other.

name: Thornwall â€” Settlement Interior
description: Inside the Palisade, Thornwall's settlement is a mix of goblin warrens and warg dens whose spatial arrangement reflects the Wargbond principle that neither species commands the other's space. Goblin-built structures of thorn-wood and clay alternate with warg den-mounds whose entrances are wide enough for a large dog but whose interiors are acoustically designed to carry scent and sound in ways that serve as an internal security system. The settlement smells of earth, fur, and cookfire.

name: Thornwall â€” Tinker's Row
description: Goblin engineering at Thornwall reaches its highest expression on Tinker's Row, where workshops produce trap mechanisms, mechanical devices, and improvised technology at a level that embarrasses some professional human artificers. Designs left here by passing Tinker Clan traders become permanent installations in the cultural archive â€” Thornwall has accumulated three generations of Tinker Clan innovation in a physical space small enough to walk in twenty minutes. The smell of oil, sap, and worked metal is distinctive.

name: Thornwall â€” Warg Den Quarter
description: The warg den quarter is a neighborhood of excavated mounds whose social organization reflects warg pack structure â€” each den housing a pack of ten to thirty individuals whose hierarchy is maintained through scent-marking, communal sleeping, and the vocal communication that sounds alarming to non-wargs but constitutes nuanced social language. Non-warg visitors are warned not to enter den-mounds uninvited and are expected to announce their presence when approaching â€” the warg early-warning system here is extremely sensitive.

name: Thornwall â€” Howl Circle
description: The cleared central space of Thornwall where warg packs gather for spiritual ceremonies â€” communal singing, story-sharing, and dispute resolution through feats of endurance. The circle's ground is packed to hardness by generations of paw-falls, and carved totems at the circle's four compass points represent the pack qualities the Wargbond values: speed, endurance, loyalty, and the clarity of sound. Kin visitors are welcome to observe but must be formally invited to participate â€” an invitation that, once given, carries genuine weight.

name: Thornwall â€” Bondfeast Hall
description: The Bondfeast Hall is the physical expression of the Wargbond's founding principle â€” goblins and wargs eat together, neither commanding the other. The hall's entrance is carved with the settlement's motto: 'Neither chain nor collar.' The hall is long and low, built to accommodate both bipedal goblins seated at tables and quadrupedal wargs who prefer to eat on the floor â€” both arrangements are provided without hierarchy. The food is simple, abundant, and shared.

name: Thornwall â€” Co-Chiefs' Hall
description: Kex Ironteeth and Silverfang govern Thornwall jointly from this hall, whose furniture arrangement requires no explanation: one side has a goblin-scaled desk and chair; the other has a raised platform where Silverfang can be seated at eye-level with a standing goblin. Joint leadership by consensus means every significant decision is made here twice â€” once in Goblin, once in Warg-tongue â€” before it is enacted. The process is slower than autocracy and more legitimate than alternatives the Marches have seen.

name: Thornwall â€” Outer Warg Dens
description: The outermost ring of warg dens serves as both residential space and the settlement's first defensive layer â€” wargs sleeping in these dens detect intruder-scents well outside the Palisade's physical boundary and their alarm-howls give the settlement minutes of warning before any physical threat arrives. The dens here are larger than the inner ring, and the packs that occupy them are specifically chosen for alertness and reliability rather than social seniority.

name: Thornwall â€” Goblin Engineer Workshops
description: The engineering workshops where Thornwall's goblin population maintains and extends the settlement's defenses are less chaotic than they appear â€” the apparent disorder of partial projects and component piles follows a logic that goblin engineers can navigate at a run. Current projects include an extension to the outer Palisade, a new trap mechanism for the main gate's approach corridor, and an ongoing negotiation with the warg packs about the placement of a signal-drum system that would complement their scent-alert capacity.

name: Thornwall â€” South Approach Trap Field
description: The ground between Thornwall's outer Palisade and the jungle tree-line to the south is not neutral territory â€” it is a goblin-engineered trap field whose contents are updated regularly and whose map exists only in Kex Ironteeth's memory and the Tinker Clan records locked in his desk. Authorized visitors are escorted through on a specific path that is deliberately irregular to prevent memorization. The trap field has stopped three separate armed approaches in Thornwall's history, which goblin engineers consider a successful proof-of-concept.

name: Thornwall â€” Herb-Medicine Cache
description: Thornwall maintains a medical cache stocked primarily through trade with Canopy Hold and Murtavah Root, since neither goblin nor warg biological medicine has developed to the level of the Kin traditions. The cache is maintained by a Halfling healer who has lived in Thornwall for twelve years and considers the joint goblin-warg culture the most genuinely egalitarian society they have encountered in extensive Marches travel. Their loyalty to the settlement is genuine and complete.

name: Thornwall â€” Trade Post
description: Thornwall's trade post handles the exchange of tinker-work, trap mechanisms, jungle guide services, and warg-scouted maps for the goods the settlement needs from outside â€” primarily food, metal stock, and the specialized materials that goblin engineering requires. Kin traders who reach Thornwall through the Palisade gate find trade here conducted with absolute fairness and an expectation of equivalent fairness in return. Attempts to cheat Thornwall are remembered and discussed in the Bondfeast Hall.

name: Thornwall â€” North Jungle Gate
description: The northern gate of Thornwall opens onto the Ash Trails, and its traffic is primarily the warg-scout patrols that maintain Thornwall's intelligence network across the surrounding Deepwood. The gate here is simpler than the main southern entrance â€” a sliding bar rather than a counterweight portcullis â€” because northern traffic is almost entirely controlled and known. Unknown visitors arriving from the north are treated with considerably more suspicion than those from the south.

name: Thornwall â€” Scent-Post Ring
description: The outer scent-post ring â€” a series of marked posts that warg packs maintain as territorial boundary indicators â€” occupies the tree-line around Thornwall. Non-warg visitors rarely notice the posts as anything but sticks; warg rangers treat them as the information-dense boundary markers they are, capable of conveying age, identity, and health status of any warg that has passed in the preceding seventy-two hours. The ring is refreshed continuously by rotating pack members.

name: Thornwall â€” Warg Elder's Den
description: The largest single warg den in Thornwall belongs to the pack elder whose age and experience give them the closest thing to a permanent advisory role in the settlement's governance. The current elder is a grey-muzzled female named Deeptrack whose scent-memory of Thornwall spans forty years and whose counsel Silverfang defers to on questions of inter-pack dynamics. Visitors to Deeptrack's den are received in a small anteroom; the elder herself is rarely visible but always present.

name: Murtavah Root â€” Jungle Approach
description: The trail to Murtavah Root is not marked. Travelers who know the way follow a series of natural indicators â€” specific bark markings, stone arrangements on the forest floor, and the increasing intensity of certain fungal smells â€” that are detailed only in guides sold at the Whisper Market. Those who arrive without a guide typically circle the Root's outer margin for hours, aware that they are close to something but unable to locate the entrance. The jungle here is dense even by Deepwood standards.

name: Murtavah Root â€” The Root Entrance
description: The massive centuries-old tree whose root system forms Murtavah Root's physical structure is visible at last â€” a trunk of impossible diameter whose exposed roots rise from the forest floor like the buttresses of a cathedral built by something other than human hands. The spaces between roots are built-into: market stalls occupy the larger gaps, meeting rooms are cut into the roots themselves, and the passages within are lit by bioluminescent fungi cultivated in the ceiling crevices. The air smells of earth, fungus, and years.

name: Murtavah Root â€” Outer Stall Ring
description: The outer ring of Murtavah Root's market occupies the spaces between the tree's largest surface roots, each stall a semi-permanent installation whose owner has negotiated placement with the Rootwarden's staff. Jungle guides, rare fungi, alchemical reagents, and preserved foods are the standard trade goods here. Information is also sold, but the more valuable grades require moving to the Whisper Market inside. The stall-keepers are a polyglot mix of the Root's demographics â€” every type of person the Marches produces.

name: Murtavah Root â€” The Whisper Market
description: Underground beneath the Root's central trunk, the Whisper Market is where information is the currency. Jungle guide maps, locations of Deceiver burrow-forts, intelligence on Flamewarg patrol patterns, and the coordinates of Giant ruins change hands at prices calibrated to the buyer's urgency and the seller's confidence in their data's accuracy. The market's name is literal â€” conversations here are conducted at speaking-voice levels that do not carry beyond the next booth. Newcomers who arrive speaking normally receive one warning.

name: Murtavah Root â€” The Root (Inner Chamber)
description: The inner chamber of the Root is a space carved from living wood â€” the tree's own interior, shaped over decades by whatever the Rootwarden is. The walls breathe. This is not metaphor: the wood is still alive, and seasonal growth and contraction make the room's dimensions slightly different in every season. The Rootwarden governs from here, visible occasionally as a silhouette in the chamber's deeper reaches. The identity of the current Rootwarden is genuinely unknown outside a small inner circle.

name: Murtavah Root â€” West Herb Quarter
description: The western section of the Root's market specializes in herbal remedies â€” the most legitimate and least controversial of the Root's exports. Pasua and Mixed Human herbalists work drying-racks and grinding stations among the outer roots, and the smell of medicinal herbs gives this section a completely different character from the Whisper Market's earthier atmosphere. Many of the herbs sold here are genuinely unavailable anywhere north of the Infinite Desert.

name: Murtavah Root â€” The Veil Grove
description: A secluded clearing adjacent to the Root's main structure, accessible only through the Whisper Market's rear passage, is where the Deceiver's Feat is taught to those the Rootwarden deems worthy. The practice â€” concealing one's Kin-sense signature, making oneself invisible to the elemental resonance that defines every living being in Oreka â€” is not inherently evil, but its association with the Deceivers makes this grove the most politically sensitive space in the entire Marches. The teachers here are skilled and patient and ask very few questions about motivation.

name: Murtavah Root â€” Goblin Quarter
description: The Root's ten percent goblin population occupies a cluster of burrow-residences dug beneath the tree's root-system, accessible through tunnels too small for most adult humans. The goblins here are a self-selected population â€” those who found neither Thornwall's Wargbond structure nor the deep jungle's Free Tribes suited to their temperament. They are, as a group, the most information-connected goblins in the Marches, and their intelligence network through the Whisper Market is substantial.

name: Murtavah Root â€” Rootwarden's Council Room
description: Adjacent to the inner chamber, a council room of carved root-wood serves for the Rootwarden's occasional public consultations â€” meetings with faction representatives, adjudication of trade disputes, and the rare formal audiences that the Rootwarden grants to outside parties. The room's furniture is grown rather than constructed â€” root-chairs shaped over years into functional forms. The Rootwarden has not appeared in person outside the Root in the memory of anyone currently alive in Murtavah Root.

name: Murtavah Root â€” East Jungle Margin
description: The eastern edge of Murtavah Root backs against the densest section of the surrounding Deepwood, where the tree's root-system and the surrounding jungle's ground-cover have merged into an impassable tangle that serves as a natural defensive wall. The Rootwarden's staff maintains concealed observation positions in the tangle, and the smell of the jungle here is cut by a barely detectable undertone that experienced Kin-sense practitioners associate with the kind of deliberate concealment the Veil Grove teaches.

name: The Spur â€” Peninsula Approach
description: The rocky spur of land that extends into the Apelian Sea's southern shore is visible from a kilometer away â€” a spine of dark stone jutting into the warm blue-green water with the purposeful geometry of something shaped rather than eroded. The jungle around the spur's base is unnaturally dense, and Kin-sense begins to flicker here at the approach's edge: not the dead-zone of the Glass Wastes but an active interference, directional and precise. Something ahead is generating it deliberately.

name: The Spur â€” Jungle Barrier
description: The jungle around the Spur's base is not merely dense â€” it has been shaped. Thorned growth forms walls without gaps; root systems trip and grip at ankle-height; bioluminescent fungi positioned at branch-height emit a light that confuses depth perception. Whether this arrangement is natural, deliberate, or the result of some Domnathar working that has persisted for centuries is impossible to determine from outside. Kin-sense is effectively dead here. The dead-zone presses on the mind with a quality that feels less like absence and more like being watched.

name: The Spur â€” Rock Base
description: The jungle gives way abruptly to bare rock at the Spur's base â€” a transition as sharp as a surveyor's line. The dark stone here has the specific mineral composition of Domnathar worked stone: veined with a blue-grey metal that does not correspond to any Orekan alloy and that is responsible for the Kin-sense dead-zone. The rock is warm to the touch despite the lake breeze. At the Spur's tip, the tower rises without visible entrance from water level.

name: The Spur â€” Tower Exterior
description: The Domnathar tower of dark stone stands at the Spur's tip, its surface seamless from the outside â€” no windows at lower levels, no visible mortar joints in the stone construction, no external entrance. The blue-grey metal veining in the tower's stone is more concentrated here than at the base, and the Kin-sense dead-zone is absolute. The tower's top glows with a pulsing blue-white light on some nights, visible from five miles across the lake. Up close, the stone surface is cold despite the warm climate â€” cold in a way that suggests deliberate maintenance rather than natural property.

name: The Spur â€” Tower Entry (Hidden)
description: The tower's entrance is not visible from outside â€” it requires knowledge of the specific stone sequence that activates a door mechanism built into the Domnathar metal veining. Inside the threshold, the cold is immediate and absolute: the tower maintains a temperature that the pre-invasion Domnathar house leaders find comfortable and that Kin visitors find deeply wrong in a way that is not merely physical. The interior walls carry inscriptions in a script that predates every known writing system in Oreka.

name: The Spur â€” Tower First Level
description: The tower's first interior level is a working space â€” analytical instruments of alien manufacture arranged on surfaces that appear to be cast from the same blue-grey metal as the wall-veining. The instruments monitor something: whether Kin-sense patterns, elemental resonances, or something else entirely is not determinable without the knowledge to read their displays. The Domnathar who rebuilt this tower over three centuries have had time to establish a very thorough intelligence operation. The question of what they know is the most dangerous open question in the Marches.

name: The Spur â€” Tower Second Level
description: The second level serves as residential quarters for whoever currently occupies the tower â€” the Domnathar house leaders who fled here after Dark Dawn are still alive at nearly six hundred years of age, and they have had three centuries to make their accommodation comfortable by their own standards. The furnishings are alien in proportion and aesthetic, and a library of flat metal tablets covers one wall entirely. The tablets are indexed and accessible; their content is in Domnathar script.

name: The Spur â€” Tower Upper Level
description: The upper level of the Spur Tower is where the blue-white light originates on its active nights â€” a mechanism of worked Domnathar metal that functions as a long-range communication device, sending signals in a pattern that could be received by anyone with the decryption knowledge. Whether it communicates with other Domnathar remnants, with the home world, or with something else is unknown. The mechanism is currently active in a low-power standby state and has been for the past decade.

name: The Spur â€” Water Approach (South)
description: The Spur's southern shoreline is accessible by small boat at normal water levels â€” flat rock shelves extending into the Apelian provide landing points, though the Kin-sense dead-zone extends across the water surface as well. The lake here is unusually clear, its bottom visible at depths where the Apelian is normally opaque, and the rock formations visible below the waterline continue the Domnathar architectural pattern of the Spur itself.

name: The Spur â€” Water Approach (East)
description: The eastern face of the Spur meets the Apelian in a series of natural rock shelves whose edges are sharper than weathering alone would produce. Small cave-openings at water level â€” too small for an adult human to enter but large enough to admit instruments or communications â€” are set at regular intervals in the Domnathar stone. The lake water around the Spur's eastern face is oddly still regardless of wind conditions elsewhere on the Apelian.

name: The Spur â€” North Shore Access
description: The north side of the Spur faces the main body of the Apelian, and from here on clear days the Tidewell Stiltmarket is visible as a smear above the water line. The Eelglow Wharf's nighttime glow is visible from this point on moonless nights. The Domnathar in the tower above would have had a clear line-of-sight observation post on Tidewell's activities for three centuries, a fact that Harbormaster Enna Farshore has declined to think about in detail.

name: The Spur â€” Outer Rock Shelf
description: A broad shelf of Domnathar stone extends from the tower's base into the lake, partially submerged at high water. The shelf's surface is not natural stone â€” it is worked, its edges precise and its surface level to a degree that requires tools to achieve. Whatever purpose the shelf originally served â€” loading dock, defense platform, or something for which no Kin word exists â€” it now serves primarily as the point from which the tower can be photographed from the water by curious traders who have heard the stories.

name: The Spur â€” Concealed Cache
description: A section of the tower's ground-level exterior conceals a cache of Domnathar materials behind a stone panel operated by the same metal-sequence mechanism as the main door. The cache contains items whose nature is unclear â€” sealed containers of the Domnathar metal, flat tablet-records, and what appears to be emergency equipment in a configuration suggesting preparation for rapid departure. The cache has not been accessed in at least twenty years, based on the undisturbed surface around the panel.

name: Deceiver's Refuge â€” Settlement Edge
description: The settlement at the Spur's base has the quality of a place that does not want to be found and has succeeded moderately well at the project. Buildings of local jungle materials are arranged without regular streets, and the settlement's boundaries are deliberately unclear â€” there is no gate, no wall, no point where outside definitively becomes inside. The Kin-sense interference from the Spur above extends here at reduced intensity, enough to disorient sensitive practitioners but not to fully suppress them.

name: Deceiver's Refuge â€” Central Gathering Space
description: The closest thing Deceiver's Refuge has to a public space is this clearing where the settlement's transient population â€” students of the Kin-sense concealment feat, fugitives who need to disappear, Silent Concord agents, and the permanent residents who teach and support them â€” converge for communal meals and information exchange. The atmosphere is tense with the particular social electricity of people who all have reasons to want anonymity in a space that makes anonymity structurally difficult.

name: Deceiver's Refuge â€” Teacher's Quarter
description: The teachers of the Deceiver's Feat live in this quarter of small permanent buildings at the Refuge's western edge. They are, as a group, the least anonymous people in the settlement â€” known by reputation throughout the Marches as instructors in a forbidden art. Their motivations vary: some are idealists who believe Kin-sense concealment should be democratized, some are pragmatists who teach for compensation, and at least one is almost certainly reporting to the tower above. Which one is the most dangerous open question in a settlement full of dangerous open questions.

name: Deceiver's Refuge â€” Student Residences
description: The temporary housing for students of the concealment feat is a cluster of lean-to structures and repurposed jungle shelters that changes configuration as each cohort of students arrives and departs. The student population is always mixed â€” Kin of multiple types, the occasional Farborn (who cannot learn the feat but sometimes arrive seeking its related knowledge), and individuals whose background they have chosen not to share. The residents do not ask each other's reasons for being here. This is the settlement's only consistent social rule.

name: Deceiver's Refuge â€” Practice Ground
description: The concealment feat requires a physical space for practice â€” not because the feat has physical components, but because testing whether concealment is working requires an observer with Kin-sense. The practice ground is where students stand still while instructors with Kin-sense walk circles around them, reporting on what they can detect. The ground has a strange feel: it has been the site of thousands of attempts to become undetectable, and the accumulated practice has given the air here a quality of deliberate blankness.

name: Deceiver's Refuge â€” South Waterfront
description: The Refuge has access to the Apelian through a small private landing at its southern edge, used for supply delivery and the occasional departure of students who prefer to leave by water rather than through the jungle. The landing is not secret â€” anyone on the lake can see it â€” but the approach to the Refuge by water still requires knowledge of the jungle-trail system from the landing to the settlement. Small boats tied here come from Tidewell and occasionally from further afield.

name: Deceiver's Refuge â€” Supply Cache
description: The Refuge's supply cache is stocked by the same Tidewell trade connection that supplies much of the Marches â€” medicinal herbs, preserved food, and trade goods. The cache's inventory suggests a permanent population of around forty with a transient student population that fluctuates between ten and sixty. The stockpile's size implies confidence in continued supply, which implies continued trade relationships, which implies that Tidewell knows exactly where the Refuge is and has decided to treat the knowledge as a trade matter rather than a security matter.

name: Deceiver's Refuge â€” Watcher's Post
description: An elevated position in a large tree at the Refuge's eastern margin gives line-of-sight toward the Spur Tower above. A permanent observer here watches the tower with a regularity that suggests either coordination with the tower's inhabitants or very careful monitoring of them. The post is staffed continuously by members of the Refuge's permanent population â€” whoever they are keeping watch for, they take the responsibility seriously.

name: Deceiver's Refuge â€” Information Broker's Stall
description: One permanent resident of the Refuge operates what is politely described as an information brokerage â€” buying and selling intelligence about Marches conditions, Flamewarg movements, and the locations of Deceiver burrow-forts. The connection to the Whisper Market at Murtavah Root is acknowledged and functional, and the price differential between the two markets reflects the Refuge's smaller customer base and more specialized product mix. Whether the broker is a Silent Concord asset, an independent operator, or something else is the kind of question the Whisper Market charges premium prices to answer.

name: Deceiver's Refuge â€” Deep Concealment Practice Room
description: An interior room â€” rare in a settlement of outdoor structures â€” has been built for the advanced practice of Kin-sense concealment at close range, where the challenge is greatest. The room is dark and enclosed, removing visual and audio cues, forcing the Kin-sense instructor to work with pure elemental resonance. Students who progress to this room have already passed the outdoor practice stage and are learning to maintain concealment under conditions that a skilled Kin-sense reader would create specifically to defeat it.

name: Deceiver's Refuge â€” North Jungle Margin
description: The northern edge of the Refuge abuts the deepest section of the surrounding Deepwood, and the permanent residents have established a perimeter of awareness rather than a physical barrier â€” knowing the jungle so well that the sound of anything larger than a bird moving through the undergrowth registers immediately. The Flamewarg patrols that haunt the jungle margins avoid the Refuge's northern approach with a consistency that suggests either a standing agreement or a very well-publicized warning.

name: Drowned Piers â€” Shore Approach
description: The northern shore of the Apelian Sea east of Tidewell shows the first signs of the Drowned Piers at low water â€” dark stone tips breaking the surface in rows, their spacing too regular and their forms too angular to be natural reef. The underwater structures extend fifty meters into the lake at this section, their full extent mapped by Halfling pearl-divers who have worked these waters for generations. The water here is unnaturally clear despite the Apelian's normal mineral turbidity.

name: Drowned Piers â€” Shallow Approach
description: Wading into the Apelian at the pier site, the first Giant stone structures become visible underfoot â€” paving of massive slabs, the tops of submerged walls, and the rune-carved foundations of structures whose upper portions collapsed or were removed in the centuries since the piers were built. The water is warm, clear, and apparently shallow for the first twenty meters; then the bottom drops sharply to depths requiring full swimming. Luminescent eels drift around the base structures, their glow visible even in daylight.

name: Drowned Piers â€” Main Pier (Submerged)
description: The main pier extends into the Apelian at a depth of five to eight meters, its Giant-carved surface still bearing the mooring rings where the lake barges of the Giant era were tied. The rings are four feet in diameter â€” appropriate for the scale of craft the Giants operated. Rune-circles set into the pier's surface glow with faint amber light that pulses in a pattern that has become more pronounced in recent months, drawing the water serpents that usually avoid this section of the shore.

name: Drowned Piers â€” Pier Junction (Deep)
description: Where the main pier meets a transverse pier beneath the Apelian's surface, a broad platform of Giant stonework creates a T-junction twelve meters down. The rune-circles here are the most active of the Drowned Piers system â€” the glow is constant rather than pulsing, and the elemental energy discharge is sufficient to be felt as pressure in the ears by divers who remain too long. Three water serpents circle the junction in a formation that looks organized rather than coincidental.

name: Drowned Piers â€” South Pier (Deep)
description: The southern transverse pier descends as it extends outward, reaching fifteen meters at its far end before the stone structure ends in a deliberate terminus â€” not a break but a designed edge, the structure complete as built. The terminus platform shows Giant-script inscription along its outer edge, partially legible to those with the relevant language skill. Pearl-divers have reported that items left on the terminus during a rune-circle activation pulse are sometimes found moved when the divers return â€” moved toward the rune center rather than away from it.

name: Drowned Piers â€” Submerged Warehouse (Entry)
description: A building whose function was almost certainly warehousing â€” its dimensions and the remnant cargo-hook infrastructure inside suggest it â€” sits on the lake floor directly behind the main pier, its stone walls still structurally sound despite submersion. The interior has filled with silt over centuries but the ceiling vaults are clear, creating a room of eerie stillness accessible to divers skilled enough to navigate the collapsed entry. Air pockets form in the vault tops during calm weather.

name: Drowned Piers â€” Submerged Warehouse (Interior)
description: Inside the warehouse, the silt floor is undisturbed except in one corner where previous investigators have carefully excavated around what appear to be sealed cargo containers of Giant-carved stone. The containers are too heavy to raise without specialized equipment. Three have been opened by divers who reported the contents as 'preserved materials of unknown type' before the site was placed under Tideshrine supervision pending formal documentation. The documentation has not yet been completed.

name: Drowned Piers â€” Rune-Circle Core
description: At the deepest accessible point of the Drowned Piers, the primary rune-circle of the Giant-era pier complex covers the entire floor of a circular chamber. The circle's glow at this depth is not amber but a deeper orange-gold, and its pulse rate has accelerated measurably in the past month. Water serpents enter the chamber freely but do not approach the circle's carved channels. The elemental energy here is strong enough that water magic spells activated within the chamber affect a doubled area without additional effort from the caster.

name: Drowned Piers â€” Pier Terminus Chamber
description: Below the terminus platform of the south pier, a chamber cut into the lake bed â€” not a natural formation but an excavated room of Giant construction â€” holds what appears to be a secondary communication or storage point for the pier complex. The walls carry Giant-script in a formal register different from the operational inscriptions on the pier surfaces above â€” more deliberate, more spaced, and, to those who can partially read it, unmistakably archival in character.

name: Drowned Piers â€” Eel Nursery
description: A section of the Drowned Piers that the pearl-divers avoid is claimed by a colony of luminescent eels whose young are raised in the protected spaces between submerged stones. The eels' bioluminescence is brightest here, making the underwater section glow blue-green even in daylight visible from boats above. Adult eels are non-aggressive except in defense of the nursery, and pearl-divers who navigate through rather than around this section occasionally report being escorted by adult eels for several meters before the escort turns back.

name: Drowned Piers â€” Surface Observation Raft
description: A Tideshrine-maintained observation raft is anchored over the main pier site, staffed by two shrine priests who monitor the rune-circle pulse activity and the water serpent behavior above the pier system. The raft has a glass-bottom viewing section â€” rare technology, a gift from the Rarozhki gem-cutters â€” that allows limited observation of the shallow sections without diving. The priests' logs of the past six months show a consistent acceleration in rune activity that they have not yet been able to explain.

name: Ashcrown Warren â€” Jungle Approach
description: The jungle around the Ashcrown Warren site shows signs of its history â€” fire-cleared sections that grew back with the dense secondary growth characteristic of burned forest, and isolated patches of alien vegetation that appeared after the Deceiver warbands established their burrow-forts here. The ground underfoot is hollow-sounding in sections, indicating the tunnel network below. Hobgoblin patrol marks â€” slashed bark at head-height â€” warn the boundary of the contested section.

name: Ashcrown Warren â€” Outer Burrow Entrance
description: The outer burrow entrance of the Ashcrown Warren is a tunnel mouth of Deceiver-built stone construction, its entrance wider than Kin engineering would produce and its frame bearing alien geometric decoration. Hill tribe goblins have established a camp at the entrance â€” whether as occupiers or squatters is a distinction they consider irrelevant. The tunnel beyond descends at an angle and curves out of sight within twenty feet, and the sound from its depths is an irregular echo that suggests occupied spaces further in.

name: Ashcrown Warren â€” Upper Tunnel Level
description: The upper level of the Ashcrown Warren's tunnel network is partially collapsed â€” sections of the original Deceiver construction have failed over the centuries, and the hill tribe goblins who occupy the upper sections have propped the worst areas with jungle-wood supports that are structurally inferior to the original stonework. The ceiling is low enough to require Kin visitors to duck in the collapsed sections, and the air is close and smells of earth, cookfire, and goblin occupation.

name: Ashcrown Warren â€” Goblin Occupation Zone
description: The section of the Warren occupied by hill tribe goblins is identifiable by the accumulated debris of occupation â€” fire-pits, sleeping areas of leaf-mat, tool-caches, and the incompletely preserved remains of meals. The goblins here are not the Wargbond communities of Thornwall â€” these are wild-tribe goblins whose social structure is more fluid and whose relationship to the Warren is opportunistic rather than principled. They tolerate visitors who demonstrate no threat and show interesting trade goods.

name: Ashcrown Warren â€” Sealed Section
description: A heavy door of Deceiver technology â€” not stone but the same alien metal-composite used in the Spur Tower â€” seals the deeper sections of the Warren from the upper levels. The goblins have not opened it in living memory and show no interest in doing so; whatever they heard from the deeper levels at some point convinced them that the upper Warren was sufficient. The door's locking mechanism is alien in design and resists all conventional opening attempts.

name: Ashcrown Warren â€” Hobgoblin Patrol Post
description: A hobgoblin patrol maintains a post at the Warren's eastern surface entrance â€” a secondary access point that the Deceiver-descent hobgoblins consider their territorial boundary marker. The patrol rotation suggests three to four active combatants operating from a larger group based deeper in the Warren. Their equipment is a mixture of Kin-made and alien â€” the hobgoblins who descend from the original Deceiver warbands maintained access to some alien manufacturing processes for several generations before those capacities finally failed.

name: Ashcrown Warren â€” Lower Tunnel (Goblin Access)
description: The lower tunnel section is accessible from the upper level through a narrow shaft that the goblins have further narrowed with debris to prevent easy descent by anything their size or larger. The lower level's construction is noticeably better than the upper â€” original Deceiver stonework maintained by whatever process the alien builders used, showing no deterioration despite centuries of submersion in jungle moisture. The air below is cooler and carries a metallic smell.

name: Ashcrown Warren â€” Sealed Deep Level
description: The deepest accessible section of the Ashcrown Warren is a network of Deceiver-built chambers whose original purpose is illegible from current evidence â€” the equipment they contained has been removed or destroyed, leaving only the spatial organization of the rooms, which suggests functions without identifying them. One chamber has a floor marking that corresponds to the same geometric pattern found in the Spur Tower's first level. Whether the connection is functional or merely architectural is unknown.

name: Ashcrown Warren â€” Collapsed Section
description: A significant section of the Warren's tunnel network has collapsed, the weight of the jungle above overcoming the alien stonework's capacity. The collapse zone extends for approximately thirty meters and is impassable without excavation. Judging by the settlement of the debris, the collapse occurred several decades ago and was not repaired â€” whether because the Warren's occupants lacked the capacity or because they considered whatever was on the other side of the collapse more safely isolated than accessible is unclear.

name: Ash Trail â€” Western Junction
description: A network junction on the Ash Trails marks the divergence of routes toward Titan's Rest to the east, Thornwall to the north, and the deeper Deepwood to the west and south. The burned-path surface is wide enough here for a wagon, and the clearing that marks the junction shows evidence of regular use: flattened grass, scattered charcoal from cooking fires, and the deliberately maintained absence of new growth that characterizes the Ash Trails' active sections.

name: Ash Trail â€” North Track to Thornwall
description: The trail north toward Thornwall runs through secondary jungle growth â€” lighter canopy than the old-growth Deepwood, moving faster but less concealing. Warg-scout waymarks appear at intervals: bark-scratches at nose-height for a walking human that translate to specific directional and condition information in warg trail-language. Travelers who can read them have a navigational advantage; travelers who cannot should follow the burned surface and not leave it.

name: Ash Trail â€” West Track (Deep Jungle)
description: The western trail leads into old-growth Deepwood where the canopy blocks ninety percent of sunlight and the forest floor is in permanent twilight. The Ash Trail burned surface becomes less reliable here â€” new growth reclaims it faster in the deep forest and the maintenance gap between burnings is sometimes long enough for a season's growth to obscure the path. Survival and Navigation checks are higher here, and the appropriate wildlife is larger.

name: Ash Trail â€” South Track to Tidewell
description: The southern Ash Trail toward Tidewell and the Apelian shore follows a route that Flamewarg cults have used extensively â€” the burned sections here were wider than necessary for simple passage, suggesting fire-clearing operations that were expansive rather than precise. Charcoal deposits are deeper and older than elsewhere on the network. Caravans traveling this section prefer to move in groups and to move during daylight.

name: Ash Trail â€” Flamewarg Territory Margin
description: The jungle here shows the characteristic signature of Flamewarg cult passage â€” fire-cleared sections that have grown back with the dense secondary growth of burned forest, isolated patches of unusual vegetation, and the disturbing silence that means local wildlife has been displaced by a predator it respects. The Ash Trail through this section is the most ambush-suitable in the network, and caravan masters charge premium rates for their guide service on this specific stretch.

name: Ash Trail â€” Canopy Hold Approach Branch
description: A branch of the Ash Trail system runs northeast from the south track toward Canopy Hold, identifiable by the Kovaka navigation markers â€” small stone cairns at knee-height â€” that replace the simple burned-path convention of the main trails. The markers indicate not just direction but trail condition, and a Kovaka ranger reading them can determine whether the route ahead has been recently patrolled. The trail narrows as it approaches Canopy Hold's Rootwall.

name: Ash Trail â€” River Crossing Point
description: One of the Marches' few reliable water sources on the Ash Trail network is this river crossing â€” a tributary of the Apelian system, thigh-deep at normal flow and waist-deep in wet season. A rope crossing guide maintained by Thornwall's goblin engineers provides a handhold for the crossing and a flood-alert system â€” a specific knot arrangement on the guide-rope signals when upstream conditions are deteriorating. The crossing is the social chokepoint of the western trail network, and travelers often encounter each other here who would not meet anywhere else in the Marches.

name: Ash Trail â€” Murtavah Root Approach
description: The approach trail toward Murtavah Root leaves the main Ash Trail network through a section where the burned surface disappears entirely and the navigation reverts to the natural indicators described in Root-sourced guide maps. The shift is deliberate â€” maintained by whoever maintains the Root's boundaries â€” and travelers without a guide or a current map from the Whisper Market will walk in circles here for hours without making progress toward the settlement.

name: Ash Trail â€” Ruined Waystation
description: A waystation on the old pre-Dark Dawn trail network has survived in a ruined state â€” stone walls still standing to three feet, the roof long since gone, but the fire-hearth in the center still usable. Travelers camp here regularly, and the ruins' walls provide some shelter and the illusion of structure in otherwise structureless jungle. A cache of emergency supplies hidden in the hearth's false bottom has been restocked by unknown parties at irregular intervals for longer than anyone can establish.

name: Ash Trail â€” Deepwood Crossing
description: A section of the trail where old-growth trees of extraordinary size have been burning-cleared â€” the oldest charcoal deposits here represent the founding of the Ash Trail network itself, when Flamewarg cults first cleared the corridors that later became Marches infrastructure. The trees adjacent to the burned section are ancient enough that their lower branches are twenty feet above the trail surface, creating a natural archway. The trail is well-maintained in this section by heavy use.

name: Ash Trail â€” Observation Hill
description: A natural rise in the terrain â€” rare in the Marches' otherwise flat jungle floor â€” provides the best overland sightline in the Ash Trail network. Titan's Rest's Lone Titan is visible to the east on clear days, and the Apelian's blue-green surface is visible to the south.
