Canopy Hold's Deepwatch Spire has used this hill as a reference point for its long-range observation; signal mirrors here can reach both the Spire and Titan's Rest simultaneously.

name: Ash Trail â€” Free Goblin Camp Site
description: A Free Goblin clan â€” unaffiliated with either Thornwall's Wargbond or any Deceiver-descent hierarchy â€” maintains a semi-permanent camp at this Ash Trail junction. The clan trades jungle guide services and deep-forest intelligence for food, metal goods, and the quality that distinguishes Free Tribes from all other goblin communities: the right to be left alone. They are not hostile to strangers who demonstrate respect and carry trade goods; they are entirely hostile to strangers who demonstrate anything else.

name: Ash Trail â€” Flamewarg Fire-Site
description: Evidence of a recent Flamewarg raid is visible here â€” burned sections wider than trail maintenance, and the remains of a caravan camp where the fire-damage pattern indicates attack rather than accident. The site is three to four days old by the growth of new vegetation pushing through the ash. Nothing of value remains. The attack pattern is consistent with Flamewarg fire-and-ambush tactics: fire to panic, ambush to capture, retreat to deep jungle before response can organize.

name: Ash Trail â€” Northern Network Junction
description: The northern Ash Trail network junction connects the routes to Thornwall, the Deepwood interior, and the Eternal Steppe margin. This is the Marches' most remote Ash Trail intersection, and its maintenance reflects the reduced traffic â€” the burned surface is narrower and the waymarking less frequent. Farborn Gilded scouts are the most common Kin presence in this section, their patrol circuits extending north from Titan's Rest into the steppe margin.

name: Ash Trail â€” Wargbond Scout Post
description: Thornwall maintains scout posts at intervals on the northern Ash Trail network â€” not buildings but prepared positions, each with a small supply cache and a signal system using the same bark-mark language as the warg waymarks. The post here is current â€” the bark-marks are fresh â€” indicating that a warg scout has passed within the past twenty-four hours. Whether the scout was inbound or outbound from Thornwall is determinable from the mark's direction.

name: Ash Trail â€” Steppe Transition
description: The jungle thins here at the Deepwood's northeastern margin, the dense old-growth giving way to lighter woodland that grades into the scrub and grassland of the Eternal Steppe within a kilometer. The Ash Trail burned surface ends here â€” fire-clearing is inappropriate in the drier steppe vegetation â€” and the trail becomes a simple track marked by stone cairns. The temperature rises noticeably in the transition zone, the humidity dropping from Deepwood saturation to steppe aridity in a distance that is disorienting after days in the canopy.

name: Ash Trail â€” Deepwood Interior Track
description: A track into the Deepwood's genuine interior â€” old-growth forest untouched by Flamewarg burning or Ash Trail maintenance â€” diverges from the network here, marked only by the absence of burn-clearing and the sudden oppressive increase in canopy density. The track is used by Wargbond deep-jungle traders and, reportedly, by the Rootwarden's messengers. It is not recommended for parties without a jungle guide, specific purpose, and genuine familiarity with what 'things not Kin' means in practical terms.

name: Ash Trail â€” Giant Ruin Marker
description: A Kovaka cairn-marker more elaborate than the standard trail waypoints indicates the nearby presence of a Giant ruin site â€” one of dozens in the Deepwood that have been located but not formally excavated. The ruin itself is fifty meters off-trail, its dark stone foundation visible as a geometry of straight edges amid the organic chaos of the jungle floor. Kovaka rangers who patrol this section have established a hands-off protocol: the ruin is documented but not disturbed, pending a formal survey that has been pending for twelve years.

name: Ash Trail â€” Water Serpent Crossing
description: A shallow lake-tributary crossing where water serpents have established a territorial claim. The serpents are not aggressive toward careful crossers, but careless entry into the water â€” particularly between dawn and mid-morning when the serpents feed â€” provokes defensive responses. Local knowledge includes a specific crossing point twenty meters upstream where the serpents do not territory-hold, information that the Free Goblin clan at the nearby camp site sells with complete reliability at a price commensurate with its practical value.

name: Ash Trail â€” Canopy Bridge
description: A segment of the Ash Trail crosses a section where the ground-level route is impassable in wet season â€” seasonal flooding converts a shallow dry depression into a temporary lake. The Pasua community of Canopy Hold has maintained a bridge through the canopy at this point, sixty feet up, connecting large trees on either side. The bridge is accessible by ladder at the trail's edge and navigable with reasonable confidence on the upper section â€” it is wide enough for a laden pack animal, though convincing the pack animal to use it is a separate negotiation.

name: Ash Trail â€” South Track Junction to Spur
description: A barely maintained track diverges from the south Ash Trail here, heading east toward the Spur and the Deceiver's Refuge. The track is not burned â€” fire-clearing near the Spur's Kin-sense dead zone has been unreliable in outcome, and Flamewarg cults avoid the entire area with a consistency that caravan masters read as a reliable deterrent signal. The track is used primarily by those with specific purpose at the Refuge, and those who travel it without that purpose tend to find the experience educational.

name: Apelian Shore â€” Tidewell North Beach
description: The Apelian's northern shore north of Tidewell is a stretch of warm, shallow water over sand and mud, fringed with the mangrove growth that characterizes the lake's margins. Halfling fisher-children work the shallows in the morning with hand-nets, and the shore path runs between the mangrove roots and the water edge in a route that shifts seasonally as the mangroves advance and recede. Luminescent eels are visible in the water after dark, making the shore glow faintly blue-green.

name: Apelian Shore â€” Pearl-Diving Grounds
description: The pearl-diving grounds of the Verdant Shoals extend here in a zone of shallow water above a rich mussel bed. Halfling divers work in pairs, one surfacing while the other dives in a rotation that maintains continuous harvest pressure without exhausting the bed. The pearls produced here â€” lake pearls, not salt-water â€” have a distinctive iridescence that commands premium prices in northern markets where they are rare. The diving season runs eight months of the year; in the four wet months the Shoals are inaccessible.

name: Apelian Shore â€” North Shore Reed Beds
description: Dense medicinal reed beds occupy the Apelian's northern shore at this point, extending into the shallows in a tangle of root and stem that makes boat navigation impossible and foot navigation impractical. Halfling reed-gatherers work the margins from specific access points they have maintained through the reed beds for generations, their paths invisible to those without the specific local knowledge. The reeds themselves are the source of the bulk medicinal exports that Tidewell trades north through the portage.

name: Apelian Shore â€” Western Shoals
description: The western Verdant Shoals are the richest pearl-bearing zone of the Apelian's northern shore â€” warm, shallow, and fed by the mineral-rich outflow of the lake's deeper sections. The floating islands of matted vegetation that characterize the Shoals shift position seasonally, requiring divers and fishers to re-map their working grounds each harvest season. This re-mapping is a communal activity treated as a ritual rather than a practical exercise, undertaken collectively with specific protocols that the Halfling maritime tradition maintains.

name: Apelian Shore â€” Mangrove Labyrinth
description: The Apelian's northern shore is most inaccessible at this section â€” the mangrove growth has created a labyrinth of channels, root arches, and floating root-mats that extends several hundred meters from open water to dry shore. Navigation requires either local knowledge or significant Survival skill. The labyrinth is one of the primary habitats of the water serpents that feed in the Shoals, and the serpents' young are raised in the sheltered root-spaces where predation pressure from the lake's deeper species cannot reach.

name: Apelian Shore â€” Eastern Shoals (Deep)
description: The eastern edge of the Verdant Shoals transitions to deeper water where the lakebed drops sharply and the pearl-bearing mussel beds give way to the open-water habitat of the larger freshwater fish that Tidewell's commercial fishers pursue. The boundary between shoals and deep water is navigated with precision by experienced fishers who know exactly where the mussel beds end and the water serpent territory begins. The boundary moves seasonally in ways that the fishing community tracks collectively.

name: Apelian Shore â€” Halfling Shore Camp
description: A Halfling seasonal camp on the accessible section of the northern mangrove shore serves as the base for the western Shoals' pearl-diving operations. The camp is built on a section of raised ground above the normal flood line and structured to be rebuilt quickly when the wet season's flooding inevitably requires dismantling. The families who work from this camp have done so for six generations, and the site's specific name â€” in Halfling maritime dialect â€” translates approximately as 'the place we always come back to.'

name: Apelian Shore â€” North Shore Headland
description: A low rocky headland provides the only elevated viewpoint on the Apelian's otherwise flat northern shore. From here the full sweep of the lake's western basin is visible â€” the Verdant Shoals in its iridescent shallows, the Eelglow Wharf's daytime smoke, and on the clearest days the faint dark line of the Spur on the southern horizon. The headland is a navigational landmark used by Halfling boat traffic as a reference point for cross-lake courses.

name: Apelian Shore â€” Lake Fisher Station
description: A Pasua fishing station on the eastern shore section provides access to the deeper open-water fishing grounds north of the Shoals. Pasua fishing culture here is boat-centric rather than shore-focused â€” the long canoes are suited to deeper water and greater range than the Halfling flat-bottomed skiffs that work the shallows. The two fishing cultures operate in adjacent but non-overlapping zones with an efficiency that results from long-established territorial understanding rather than formal agreement.

name: Apelian Shore â€” Storm Shelter Cove
description: A natural cove in the headland's lee provides shelter for lake traffic caught by the sudden squalls that move across the Apelian during wet season. The cove's entrance is narrow enough that wave action is significantly reduced inside, and a Tidewell-maintained chain of mooring rings allows six medium-sized boats to shelter simultaneously. The cove's inner wall has a carved list of storm dates going back two centuries â€” a Halfling maritime tradition of recording significant weather events.

name: Apelian Shore â€” Reed-Cutter's Landing
description: The primary landing point for the commercial reed harvest from the western Shoals' reed beds, this section of shore has been cleared and stabilized with driven stakes to create a firm unloading area. Reed bundles are offloaded here and sorted by grade before overland transport to Tidewell's Reed Market. The landing is busy during harvest season and quiet for the remaining four months, and the seasonal contrast in activity gives the location a boom-and-bust quality out of proportion to its modest scale.

name: Apelian Shore â€” Floating Island Anchorage
description: One of the Verdant Shoals' floating islands of matted vegetation has been stable enough in its position that Halfling traders have established a temporary market on it. The market operates during the calm season when the island's position is predictable and the vegetation mat is dense enough to support commercial traffic. The market specializes in pearl-fresh goods â€” pearls sold before formal grading, eel-oil in bulk, and the fresh fish that cannot wait for the journey to Tidewell without preservation.

name: Apelian Shore â€” Eel-Harvest Station
description: Luminescent eels are harvested here at the eastern edge of the Shoals â€” caught in specific traps rather than nets, since net-capture damages the organs responsible for bioluminescence and reduces the oil quality. The trap-and-release approach for substandard specimens is a Halfling maritime practice that has maintained the eel population at commercial-harvest levels for generations. The station smells intensely of eel-oil and the specific mineral character of Apelian shore mud.

name: Apelian Shore â€” Water Serpent Observatory
description: A Tideshrine-associated observation point on the shore tracks the water serpent population patterns of the Verdant Shoals â€” population size, movement corridors, and the behavioral changes around the Drowned Piers rune-circle that the shrine priests consider potentially significant. The observer is a Pasua elf whose family has maintained the post for three generations, accumulating a behavioral data set on the serpent population that is more comprehensive than anything in the formal scholarly literature.

name: Apelian Shore â€” Southern Shore Outlook
description: The southernmost accessible point of the Apelian's northern shore provides a view across the lake's wide southern basin toward the distant Spur. On moonless nights the Spur Tower's occasional blue-white glow is visible as a pulse on the southern horizon, and the Eelglow Wharf's constant glow provides orientation for open-lake navigation. The water here is the deepest accessible from the northern shore, and the fishing is correspondingly richer for those willing to work the depth.

name: Lone Titan Approaches â€” Eastern Base
description: The eastern base of the Lone Titan marks the boundary between the Eternal Steppe and the Marches proper. The mountain rises from essentially flat ground with a geological abruptness that still strikes geologists as anomalous â€” no surrounding range, no foothills, just steppe and then mountain. The Farborn who have lived here for three centuries have built a network of watch posts and trail markers on the eastern approach, and any caravan arriving from the steppe is seen hours before it arrives.

name: Lone Titan Approaches â€” North Trail
description: The northern approach to Titan's Rest runs along the Lone Titan's northern slope, a more exposed route than the western road but faster for travelers arriving from the northern steppe margin. Gilded scout patrol waymarks appear at regular intervals, and a signal mirror post on an intermediate ridge can relay arrival information to the city's North Watch in under five minutes. The slope here is covered in the mountain herbs that the Titanbreed horses prize and whose distinctive smell identifies the Lone Titan from several miles away on the right wind.

name: Lone Titan Approaches â€” South Trail
description: The southern trail descends from Titan's Rest toward the Ash Trail network and the Deepwood, its surface transitioning from the mountain's hardpack to the jungle margin's softer ground over the course of an hour's walk. The Dusk Guard patrols this route regularly â€” the southern approach is the most tactically concerning for Titan's Rest's defense planners, combining jungle concealment with proximity to the lake and the Spur. The trail is well-maintained but the military presence is visible.

name: Lone Titan Approaches â€” North Lookout
description: A lookout position on the Lone Titan's northern face commands an exceptional view of the steppe margin and the northern Ash Trail approaches. This is the position from which the Gilded first spotted the Deceiver scatter heading southwest from the Dark Dawn battlefield three centuries ago â€” though the current lookout platform is a much newer installation than the historical event that made the position significant. Gilded scouts who man this post know the story and feel its weight.

name: Lone Titan Approaches â€” Western Terrace Road
description: The primary approach to Titan's Rest from the west follows a terrace road cut into the Lone Titan's lower western slope, its grade carefully managed to allow loaded cargo wagons to make the ascent without requiring additional draft animals. The terraced farms visible on either side of the road produce the mountain herbs and steppe-wool that form the domestic basis of the Farborn economy, and the farmers who work them have the alert practicality of people who live one defensive perimeter outside the city proper.

name: Lone Titan Approaches â€” Titanbreed Grazing Range
description: An extensive section of the Lone Titan's lower slope is devoted to the open grazing range where Titanbreed horses that are not in active training or the sale-preparation process are kept on the mountain's natural forage. The herbs that grow on the Lone Titan â€” some species found nowhere else on Oreka â€” are believed to contribute to the Titanbreed's exceptional health characteristics, and the grazing range is managed with the same meticulous attention as the breeding stables.

name: Lone Titan Approaches â€” Giant's Teeth Overlook
description: From a secondary ridge on the Lone Titan's northeast face, the distant peaks of the Giant's Teeth are visible on clear days â€” the northern mountain range that feeds the Great River and marks the Infinite Desert's northern boundary. Between the Lone Titan and the Giant's Teeth, the Eternal Steppe spreads as a sea of gold and brown, its surface occasionally dotted by the dust-cloud signatures of Pasua and Mytroan cavalry patrols. This view is the same view Ralven Duskharrow had when he chose this site â€” the mountain that hides, commanding the ground that stretches.

name: Ashcrown Warren -- Common Market
description: A wider chamber at the heart of the warren, where the goblins run a crude market for whatever their raiders bring home. A trestle-table doubles as a butcher's block and a display rack. A hobgoblin 'tribute collector' in Shattered Host colors sits at the far end, ticking off goods on a slate. He is ignored as a visitor who never quite leaves.

name: Ashcrown Warren -- The Broker's Salon
description: A room that does not belong in a goblin warren. The walls are hung with good tapestries, the floor is laid with layered rugs, and a small iron-bound writing-desk sits under a lacquered reading-lamp. A Half-Dómnathar broker in traveling silks sits behind the desk, turning over a ledger. Two Silentborn attendants stand behind her, watching.

name: Ashcrown Warren -- Kitchen (Captive Quarters)
description: A smoke-stained kitchen where the broker's meals are prepared and the warren's stew-pot simmers. The cook is a weary human woman with a slave-collar of dark iron; she speaks only in whispers and only when she is sure no goblin is listening. She may have information worth more than any silver in this warren.

name: Ashcrown Warren -- Goblin Shaman's Shrine
description: A crudely-built shrine to a god whose carving is so rough it could be almost anything. The goblin shaman has hung crossed-out-sun banners above the altar -- the mark of the Flamewarg faith. He burns scraps of hair and paper to read the futures of his own clan, which have recently been very cloudy.

name: Ashcrown Warren -- Flamewarg Pen
description: A stinking kennel where the warren's single Flamewarg is kept between raids. The beast is a wolf-sized creature whose coat glows faintly orange at the roots, a descendant of the war-wolves the Deceivers brought through the Breach. It watches visitors with very old, very alert eyes.

name: Ashcrown Warren -- Tribute Storeroom
description: A locked strongroom where the warren's take is sorted into barrels and crates for transport to the Spur Tower. Two hobgoblins lounge by the door, eating travel-bread from a cloth and bored enough to welcome an interruption. Behind them, the goods are labeled in ink: 'FOR THE SPUR,' 'FOR THE MARKET,' and 'FOR THE FIRE.'

name: Ashcrown Warren -- Slave Pen
description: A low-ceilinged chamber with iron-barred cells along two walls. Four captives are currently held: a Pasua scout with a crude splint on her arm, two human travelers who do not yet understand what has happened to them, and a silent figure in a cloak whose face is not clearly human. Rescue is possible, but the goblins will give chase.

name: Ashcrown Warren -- Smuggling Chamber
description: A small side-vault built into the wall of the tribute storeroom, accessed only through a narrow passage. Inside, carefully wrapped in oiled silk, are the items the broker does not want the Spur Tower to know she has kept: a rack of Deceiver spell-foci, three sealed letters in her own hand, and a small jeweled pectoral of unknown provenance.

name: Ashcrown Warren -- Deep Escape Tunnel
description: A long downward-sloping passage cut by Pekakarlik hands centuries ago, re-opened by goblin work-gangs at the broker's direction. Somewhere far along this tunnel, it connects to the deep approaches of the Spur Tower. The broker uses it to move contraband. The occasional skeleton in the side-alcoves suggests she also uses it to move problems.

name: Spur Tower -- Guard Common
description: A common hall off the tower entry, dominated by a long trestle-table and a rack of pole-weapons. Hobgoblin standers-to-watch eat and rest here between shifts. The discipline is perfect; the mood is quiet and tense. No one laughs in the Spur.

name: Spur Tower -- Guard Barracks
description: Two rows of straw-stuffed pallets line the walls of this long dormitory. The floor is polished smooth by centuries of bootsteps. A small shrine-niche at one end holds a featureless black stone -- the House's god, whose name the garrison refuses to speak aloud.

name: Spur Tower -- Officers' Mess
description: A smaller dining-room for the tower's ranking hobgoblins and infiltrators. A Dómnathar officer in traveling leathers sits at the head of the table, reading a field-report by lamplight. He looks up as you enter; his eyes catch the lamp at a slightly wrong angle. He has been killing Kin scouts for three hundred years and is pleased by the prospect of more.

name: Spur Tower -- Outer Armory
description: Racks and racks of dark-iron weapons, neatly oiled and labeled. A locked cage in the back holds a half-dozen enchanted longswords and a single suit of rune-etched plate the garrison's officers rotate through as the active Champion. A weapon-smith's workbench shows a repair in progress.

name: Spur Tower -- Interrogation Cell
description: A windowless room with a single iron chair at its center, bolted to the floor. Tools of unpleasant specificity hang on hooks along one wall. A Silentborn loyalist polishes a set of small silver picks at a side-table with the patience of an artisan who does not expect to be interrupted today.

name: Spur Tower -- Archive Hall
description: A grand library-hall whose shelves climb thirty feet to a vaulted ceiling lit by slow-burning oil-lamps in recessed niches. The books are bound in dark leather, each spine bearing a small silver sigil that glows faintly in the presence of unauthorized readers. Several sigils are flickering.

name: Spur Tower -- Scribes' Room
description: A long workroom filled with slanted writing-desks where Silentborn scribes copy captured Kin texts and transliterate them into Dómnathar script. The ink-pots are elaborate; the pens are quills plucked from birds that do not exist in the Kin's taxonomies.

name: Spur Tower -- Lore-Keeper's Study
description: A private study whose walls are shelved floor-to-ceiling in leather-bound codices. A Dark Dwarf of great age sits cross-legged on a mat at the center, a book open in her lap though her eyes are milky with cataracts. Her memory supplies what her eyes no longer can. She is the living archive of three centuries of Shattered Host lineages.

name: Spur Tower -- Cipher Room
description: A small office devoted to codebreaking and cipher-work. A table in the middle is piled high with Kin military dispatches in various stages of translation. Silver decoder-rings hang on pegs along one wall. Whoever reads these knows far more about Kin plans than the Kin would prefer.

name: Spur Tower -- Forbidden Texts
description: A locked vault behind a stone door with three bronze keys set into its face. Inside, held upright in crystal cases, are the Archive's most sensitive volumes: the breeding-program logs, the genealogy of every Silentborn ever produced, and a single black-bound diary in the personal hand of the House Leader.

name: Spur Tower -- Lab Corridor
description: A long clinical corridor lined with doors that do not speak their function. The floor is tiled in white stone that shows every stain. Somewhere along this corridor something is gently weeping, and no one is coming.

name: Spur Tower -- Researcher's Workshop
description: A long counter-room whose surfaces are covered with glass apparatus, ceramic beakers, leather-bound notebooks, and rows of specimen-jars arranged by contents rather than size. A Silentborn researcher in spotless aprons bends over a microscope; she is good at her work and takes nothing personally.

name: Spur Tower -- Incubation Chamber
description: A warm, humid chamber lined with rows of heated cabinets each holding a delicate glass vessel. Inside each vessel, suspended in amber-colored solution, is a small shape the size of a thumb. They move. A Half-Dómnathar priest in hooded vestments tends the vessels with a doctor's gentle patience.

name: Spur Tower -- Cold Storage
description: A chamber kept bitterly cold by old Pekakarlik cold-stones set into the walls. Stacked on shelves are sealed glass caskets, each containing a Silentborn infant in stasis. There are perhaps forty. They are catalogued by date of extraction from the incubator. The oldest is eleven years stored; the youngest is this past spring.

name: Spur Tower -- Test Subject Holding
description: A secure ward for the Mother-Superior's long-running projects. Behind iron-barred cells, a dozen Silentborn of various ages wait in silence. Some are fully adult, still here because the Mother-Superior has not yet decided what to do with them. The Mother-Superior herself stands at the center of the ward, reviewing a clipboard.

name: Spur Tower -- Command Stair Landing
description: A modest landing at the top of the stair that climbs from the breeding-floor below. The walls here are the first bare stone the tower shows; the painted plaster of the lower levels has been replaced with plain dressed basalt, a deliberate austerity that signals seriousness of purpose.

name: Spur Tower -- Officers' Hall
description: The command floor's shared office: a long chamber with individual writing-desks along the walls, each assigned to a Half-Dómnathar officer of rank. Most sit empty at this hour; two are occupied by scholarly-looking figures who look up at intruders with polite, professional interest that does not extend to mercy.

name: Spur Tower -- Void-Sorcerer's Study
description: A circular chamber whose walls are shelved with grimoires and whose floor is inlaid with a complex silver diagram -- an astrolabe rendered in spellwork. A Dómnathar sorceress sits at the diagram's heart, one slender finger tracing a pattern that illuminates points she touches. She turns her head without surprise at your arrival.

name: Spur Tower -- Captive Scholar's Cell
description: A comfortable room with a bed, writing-desk, and shelf of hand-copied reference texts. At the desk sits an Elf in worn but clean scholar's robes. His ankles bear iron shackles with a longer-than-normal chain; he has lived here for eleven years. His captors think he is tame. He is not, quite.

name: Spur Tower -- Guard Post
description: A short corridor-chamber whose only decoration is the creature standing silent guard at its far end. An eight-foot construct of dark obsidian holds the position of parade-rest, unmoving, unbreathing. The air around it is cold. It will move when it has a reason.

name: Spur Tower -- Ante-Throne
description: A narrow hall of featureless black stone that serves as the approach to the command floor's greater chamber above. The hall is kept deliberately empty -- no chair, no decoration, no relief. Whoever walks this hall is meant to feel both the plainness of it and their own footfalls echoing.

name: Spur Tower -- Throne Stair Approach
description: The top of the narrow stair from the Command floor opens into a short columned approach. Each column is carved with a different line of Dómnathar script -- histories, oaths, curses -- so closely cut that the columns themselves seem to be speaking in a whisper just below the threshold of hearing.

name: Spur Tower -- Silence Corridor
description: A long corridor whose walls are inlaid with unfamiliar runes that absorb sound. Visitors' footfalls stop echoing after three paces; whispered words do not travel. A Dark Dwarf lore-keeper, milky-eyed and ancient, stands as the corridor's warden. An obsidian Void-Construct waits at the far end beside the throne doors. Neither speaks.

name: Spur Tower -- The Audience Chamber
description: The throne hall is larger than any other room in the tower and also the quietest. At its far end rises a low dais of black basalt, and on the dais a stone seat without ornament. Seated on the seat is a tall figure of great and terrible age, whose eyes catch the lamplight with the faint yellow of something submerged. He does not stand as intruders approach. His name is not spoken, even by his servants. He has been here four hundred and eighty-seven years, and he has been expecting this day.

name: Spur Tower -- Sanctum of the Void
description: A small private chamber adjacent to the throne, whose single feature is a basin of impossibly still black water set on a plinth. The basin does not ripple even when approached. The House Leader uses it for scrying and for silence. Dropping anything into the water results in a perfectly soundless disappearance.

name: Spur Tower -- Vault Approach
description: A heavy iron-bound door marks the entrance to the tower's private vault. The door is worked in dark iron and bears three locks, each of a different Dómnathar guild-make. Two small runes worked into the jamb will discharge upon forced entry, producing effects that vary -- the guards pool bets on which on-duty scholar's skin they will flay off.

name: Spur Tower -- The House Vault
description: A cold stone vault whose shelves hold the House's small treasures: the personal items the House Leader has kept through the centuries. A rack of pre-invasion weapons, a small chest of House-sigil rings, a sealed coffer of coinage from before the Kin knew such things, and in a crystal case on a velvet plinth, an ancient book whose title is in no living language.

name: Spur Tower -- Upper Observation
description: A narrow chamber with a single window -- the tower's only external window on this height -- looking out across the jungle canopy toward the far shore of the Apelian Sea. A brass-and-ivory spyglass on a stand is pointed at nothing in particular, but has been recently used: the dust on its footplate is disturbed.

name: Spur Tower -- Hidden Alcove
description: A small alcove behind a pivot-panel in the observation chamber, large enough for a single person to stand in comfortably. Inside is a jeweled box containing three letters in the House Leader's hand, addressed to three Dómnathar captains whose locations are not otherwise recorded. This is the last piece of information any Kin intelligence officer has ever wanted.

name: Spur Tower -- Descent Shaft
description: A narrow shaft cut straight down through the tower's core, braced by iron rungs, and leading into darkness far below the rock-base of the Spur. The descent goes deeper than any map in the Kin's possession. This is the House Leader's private route to the greater tunnels of the Shattered Host.

name: Spur Tower -- The Void Terminus
description: The bottom of the descent shaft opens into a chamber whose floor is an unpolished pool of absolutely black water. Stones dropped in make no sound; there is no splash. A passage continues beyond, leading to places that have not been mapped by anyone living. This is a dead-end for today. It will not remain one.
